title: Counterfactual pit-now value features from a one-lap game model
summary: Game-theoretic F1 work emphasizes that pit timing depends on opponent interaction, overtaking, and risk, not just self degradation.
rationale: Game-theoretic F1 work emphasizes that pit timing depends on opponent interaction, overtaking, and risk, not just self degradation. A strong feature family is the estimated gain/loss of pitting now versus staying out one more lap under simple competition assumptions.
implementation_hint: Build cheap counterfactual features: estimated lap loss if staying out one more lap from current degradation slope, estimated gain from fresh tyres by compound, position-risk proxy from current Position and Position_Change, and race-lap field medians as traffic proxies. Encode margins like `pit_now_advantage`, `one_more_lap_penalty`, and `risk_adjusted_undercut_score` using only contemporaneous or past-lap information.
expected_effect: Should capture undercut pressure and tactical urgency more directly than threshold rules tied only to expected tyre life.
risk: Race-lap aggregates are easy leakage vectors if they use future or test-context information; traffic proxies may be noisy because true gaps are absent.
